edges.description: The clonal plasma cell disorder is associated with marked overproduction of vascular endothelial growth factor.
VEGF excess increases vascular permeability and angiogenesis, producing the multiorgan features.

nodes.description: POEMS syndrome is a paraneoplastic disorder driven by an underlying clonal plasma cell neoplasm (most often a lambda-restricted monoclonal plasma cell proliferation), the upstream lesion of the disease.

evidence.reference_title: POEMS syndrome: Update on diagnosis, risk-stratification, and management.
evidence.snippet: POEMS syndrome is a life-threatening condition due to an underlying plasma cell neoplasm.

nodes.description: Markedly elevated vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) is a hallmark and major diagnostic criterion of POEMS syndrome and is central to its pathophysiology.

evidence.reference_title: POEMS syndrome: Update on diagnosis, risk-stratification, and management.
evidence.snippet: The major criteria for the syndrome are polyradiculoneuropathy, clonal plasma cell disorder, sclerotic bone lesions, elevated vascular endothelial growth factor, and the presence of Castleman disease.

nodes.description: VEGF-driven increased vascular permeability and angiogenesis underlie the extravascular volume overload (edema, effusions, ascites), organomegaly, and the broader multisystem manifestations of POEMS syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: POEMS syndrome: Update on diagnosis, risk-stratification, and management.
evidence.snippet: Minor features include organomegaly, endocrinopathy, characteristic skin changes, papilledema, extravascular volume overload, and thrombocytosis.
